From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Annual fan convention for fantasy and science fiction in Seattle, Washington, USA

| Norwescon | |
| --- | --- |
| Status | Active |
| Genre | [Science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction")/[Fantasy](/wiki/Fantasy "Fantasy") |
| Venue | DoubleTree by Hilton Hotel Seattle Airport |
| Locations | SeaTac, Washington |
| Country | USA |
| Inaugurated | 1978 |
| Attendance | 2,000 |
| Website | [Official website](https://norwescon.org) |

**Norwescon** is one of the largest regional [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction") and [fantasy](/wiki/Fantasy "Fantasy") [conventions](/wiki/Science_fiction_convention "Science fiction convention") in the United States. Located in [SeaTac](/wiki/SeaTac,_Washington "SeaTac, Washington") in [Washington](/wiki/Washington_(state) "Washington (state)") state, Norwescon has been running continuously since 1978.

"Norwescon", referring to the [Pacific Northwest](/wiki/Pacific_Northwest "Pacific Northwest") region, was also the name of the [8th World Science Fiction Convention](/wiki/8th_World_Science_Fiction_Convention "8th World Science Fiction Convention"), held in [Portland, Oregon](/wiki/Portland,_Oregon "Portland, Oregon"), in 1950.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norwescon&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![two seated people](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/2/26/Suzette_Haden_Elgin_in_2005.png/250px-Suzette_Haden_Elgin_in_2005.png)](/wiki/File:Suzette_Haden_Elgin_in_2005.png)

[Richard Stephens](/wiki/Richard_Stephens "Richard Stephens") and [Suzette Haden Elgin](/wiki/Suzette_Haden_Elgin "Suzette Haden Elgin") at Norwescon 28 in 2005

Norwescon grew out of the desire of its founders to host a [Worldcon](/wiki/Worldcon "Worldcon") in Seattle in 1981. However, there was one major issue — at the time, most of the regional fandom had little or no experience in running cons. Norwescon was therefore originally formed as an effort to get some practice for Worldcon. Realizing they would need a large group of people from which to draw volunteers, the founders also started the [Northwest Science Fiction Society](/wiki/Northwest_Science_Fiction_Society "Northwest Science Fiction Society") or NWSFS.[[1]](#cite_note-st060824-1)

The first Norwescon was held in a then-unincorporated area now known as the city of [SeaTac, Washington](/wiki/SeaTac,_Washington "SeaTac, Washington"). It featured [Theodore Sturgeon](/wiki/Theodore_Sturgeon "Theodore Sturgeon") as its Guest of Honor (GoH) and drew just over 400 attendees. The next year, Norwescon moved up the street to the Hyatt Hotel, the site of the 1961 [Worldcon](/wiki/Worldcon "Worldcon"). The Hyatt was the site of Norwescons II through VII, at which point the convention began feeling the pressures of growth; it was then moved to SeaTac's Red Lion Inn. With growth came associated problems, and after two years at the Red Lion, Norwescon was forced to find a new location on short notice. The convention moved back to the Hyatt for a convention called Alternacon. The next year brought a big move to the city of [Tacoma](/wiki/Tacoma,_Washington "Tacoma, Washington"), where the Sheraton hosted Norwescon for five years. 1993 brought the convention to the [Bellevue](/wiki/Bellevue,_Washington "Bellevue, Washington") area, and in 1994 Norwescon returned to the site of the old Red Lion Inn, now known as the DoubleTree Hotel SeaTac, where it has been ever since, excepting 2020, when the convention was canceled due to the COVID-19 pandemic, and 2021, when the convention was all-virtual.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Norwescon's Guest of Honor list has grown over the years, starting with a list of three: Writer Guest of Honor, Fan Guest of Honor, and Toastmaster. The list now includes artists, scientists, volunteers and special guests as well as many other professionals. Norwescon has always made an effort to invite guests who are deserving of the honor, and has thus had a prestigious line of guests.[[1]](#cite_note-st060824-1)

Norwescon also hosts the [Philip K. Dick Award](/wiki/Philip_K._Dick_Award "Philip K. Dick Award"), given to the author of the best original novel appearing in the previous year in paperback form without prior publication as a hardback. The first recipient of the Philip K. Dick Memorial Award was [Rudy Rucker](/wiki/Rudy_Rucker "Rudy Rucker") for his novel, *[Software](/wiki/Software_(novel) "Software (novel)")*.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Editions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norwescon&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Editions")]

By decades, reverse chronological order. XIII (13) which would've been in the early 1990s was [skipped](/wiki/Triskaidekaphobia "Triskaidekaphobia"), but since the numbering had already skipped a year for the 1987 Alternacon, it made the year count synch up with the con name again. In the early 2010s the numbering switched to [Arabic numerals](/wiki/Arabic_numerals "Arabic numerals") from [Roman numerals](/wiki/Roman_numerals "Roman numerals") before.
